name: Santomera Reservoir and Ajauque Wetland Loop
name_original: Embalse de Santomera por Fortuna — vuelta desde Albanilla
description: This moderate 29.2-mile road cycling route around Embalse de Santomera offers scenic views and varied terrain for cyclists.
region_intro: Road cycling around Abanilla offers a distinctive landscape characterized by arid desert scenery and dramatic badlands formations, often described as a "lunar landscape." The region features varied terrain, including the unexpected lushness of the Chícamo River valley and areas around thermal springs. These physical features provide a unique backdrop for road cycling routes, ranging from moderate to challenging. The area's geological formations and diverse ecosystems create a compelling environment for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Abanilla The most popular road cycling route is Santomera Reservoir and Ajauque Wetland Loop, a 29.2 miles (47.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route explores the Santomera Reservoir and the Ajauque Wetland. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alcayna–Abanilla–Fortuna Route loop from Oriental, a moderate 30.9 miles (49.7 km) path. This loop connects several towns, offering diverse scenery and a significant elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Desierto de Abanilla – Leana Spa loop from Los Baños, a 13.8 miles (22.2 km) trail leading through the unique Desierto de Abanilla, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Road cycling around Abanilla is defined by arid desert landscapes, badlands formations, and the contrasting green Chícamo River valley. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Abanilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Abanilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Abanilla?
A: Abanilla offers a diverse selection of over 15 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy to difficult, with the majority being moderate challenges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: Road cycling in Abanilla is characterized by its unique 'lunar' or 'badlands' scenery, especially in areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Abanilla&lt;/a&gt;. You'll encounter arid desert landscapes with dramatic rock formations, contrasting with the unexpected lushness around the Chícamo River and thermal springs. The terrain includes varying elevations, offering both challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Abanilla?
A: The road cycling routes in Abanilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 19 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique desert scenery, the varied terrain, and the peaceful experience away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Abanilla?
A: Yes, Abanilla offers several easy road cycling routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31527587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Abanilla – Leana Spa loop from Los Baños&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.2 km long and takes about an hour to complete, leading through the distinctive Desierto de Abanilla.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: Many of Abanilla's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009259716/santomera-reservoir-and-ajauque-wetland-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santomera Reservoir and Ajauque Wetland Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31617341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcayna–Abanilla–Fortuna Route loop from Oriental&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that offer scenic viewpoints or natural attractions?
A: Absolutely. Many routes showcase the region's natural beauty. You can cycle through the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Abanilla&lt;/a&gt;, explore the areas around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chicamo River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the thermal springs of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Baños de Fortuna&lt;/a&gt;. The region's badlands and ancient sea remnants provide stunning backdrops.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Abanilla?
A: Abanilla's climate makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer. The region's semi-desert nature means relatively mild winters, though checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: While many routes in Abanilla feature moderate to significant elevation gains, some of the easier, shorter loops can be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with some climbing. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile before heading out.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in Abanilla?
A: Many of Abanilla's cycling paths feature well-paved surfaces, making them ideal for road bikes. While the region is known for its rugged natural landscapes, the designated cycling routes generally maintain good road quality. It's always advisable to check recent route conditions if available.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Abanilla town center or near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Baños de Fortuna&lt;/a&gt;. Specific parking details can often be found in the route descriptions on komoot.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and local pubs. These spots offer opportunities to refuel and experience local hospitality. For example, routes connecting towns like Abanilla, Fortuna, and Hondón de los Frailes will have amenities available.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain in Abanilla?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, Abanilla offers routes with substantial elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27646258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Cofer – Hondón de los Frailes loop from Abanilla&lt;/a&gt; covers over 61 km with more than 640 meters of ascent, providing a demanding ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I use public transport to reach cycling routes in Abanilla with my bike?
A: Public transport options to Abanilla are limited, and policies regarding bikes can vary. It's recommended to check with local bus operators directly for their current regulations on transporting bicycles, as dedicated bike-friendly services are not widely available in this rural region.
tips: A beautiful picture that we find near the town of Fortuna along the Cobatillas - Venta Collares Highway (T-423) with a splendid asphalt and ideal for lovers of road cycling.

At this point we are at the Santomera Reservoir dam, which offers us incredible landscapes in an ideal environment for practicing any sport or photography. In the place there are several picnic areas where you can eat and / or cook. Without a doubt, one of the best places to disconnect from the city and connect with nature.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as a moderate road ride on Komoot, requiring good fitness. While the area has diverse terrain, this specific 47-kilometer route with 410 meters of elevation gain is generally manageable for road cyclists with appropriate fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Embalse de Santomera por Fortuna — return from Albanilla route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride, making it suitable for road cycling. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and could be challenging for traditional road bikes. The broader region includes arid zones, ravines, and gullies, but the primary cycling path is generally paved.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking spots for this exact route aren't detailed, the Embalse de Santomera is a popular recreational area with picnic spots, suggesting parking facilities are available nearby. You might find suitable parking in Fortuna or Abanilla to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular views of the Santomera Reservoir and the surrounding countryside. The area is a protected natural environment, featuring salt marshes, saline steppes, and a surreal landscape with desert and badlands formations, sometimes referred to as the 'Murcian Route 66'. You'll also pass through citrus groves with lemon and orange trees.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region generally offers good cycling weather for much of the year. To avoid the intense summer heat, spring and autumn are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures for a moderate ride like this one.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest or highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers scenic views of the Santomera Reservoir. You'll also ride along the Carretera Cobatillas - Venta Collares (T-423), known for its cycling suitability. The entire area is part of a protected natural environment, offering opportunities for birdwatching and enjoying the unique 'Murcian Route 66' landscape.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for cycling with dogs?
faqs.answer: While the area is a natural environment, this specific route is designed for road cycling. Bringing dogs on a road bike route can be challenging due to speed and potential traffic. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always keep them on a leash in protected areas like Humedal de Ajauque y Rambla Salada.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Humedal de Ajauque y Rambla Salada, which is a protected natural environment (Site of Community Importance and Special Protection Area for Birds). Generally, there are no entrance fees for cycling on public roads within such areas. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the route?
faqs.answer: The towns of Fortuna and Abanilla, which are part of the route's name, are likely to have cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel. The Embalse de Santomera also has picnic areas, so bringing your own snacks is an option.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for unpaved sections, we recommend bringing a well-maintained road bike, appropriate cycling gear, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and sun protection. A helmet is essential. Consider a GPS device to navigate the route effectively.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and covers 47 kilometers with over 400 meters of elevation gain, requiring good fitness. While some parts are well-paved, the length and potential for unpaved sections might make it challenging for younger children or less experienced family cyclists. Shorter, dedicated family-friendly routes might be more suitable if cycling with kids.
